## Maryland Energy Administration (MEA)
**Source:** http://energy.maryland.gov/govt/Pages/smartenergycommunities.aspx
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** 

### Local Government Energy Modernization Program (L-GEM)
- **Type:** Grant
- **Financial Details:** $64 million total: $50 million anticipated for planning activities and renewable energy projects, $10 million anticipated for energy efficiency projects, $4 million anticipated for electrification projects
- **Eligibility:** Only incorporated towns, cities, and counties of Maryland may apply. Counties that apply for AOI1 are not eligible to apply for AOI2 or AOI3.
- **How to Apply:** Application Deadline: 5:00 P.M. ET, December 5, 2025; interested applicants should check the kick-off webinar and FAQ document on the MEA website
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial, Agricultural
- **Notes:** Program closed; one-time grant opportunity for local governments to support clean energy projects

## Wellesley Municipal Light Plant (WMLP)
**Source:** http://wellesleyma.gov/799/Energy-Efficiency-Rebates
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** 

### Electrification and Efficiency Rebates
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $100, $250, $500, up to $1,500, up to $2,000, up to $3,000
- **Eligibility:** Wellesley residents with active electric accounts in good standing; new construction projects ineligible; National Grid gas customers not eligible for WMLP rebates for certain equipment
- **How to Apply:** Online form or mail-in PDF version with supporting documentation (receipt or invoice, yellow Energy Guide label)
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial
- **Notes:** Update Effective 1/1/26: 75% up to $2,000; 75% up to $3,000 if weatherization is completed within 6 months of installing a qualifying whole-home heat pump system

### Heat Pump Program
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Eligibility:** Wellesley residents with active electric accounts in good standing; new construction projects ineligible
- **How to Apply:** Link to online form or mail-in PDF version
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial

### Bring Your Own Charger (BYOC) Program
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $250; $125 for EV chargers purchased 1/1/26 or later
- **Eligibility:** Wellesley residents with active electric accounts in good standing; National Grid gas customers eligible
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial

### MassSave Rebates (referenced on WMLP page)
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Eligibility:** National Grid gas customers eligible for rebates and not WMLP rebates; new construction projects ineligible
- **How to Apply:** Link to online form or mail-in PDF version
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial

**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** 

### Commercial & Industrial Grant Program
- **Type:** grant
- **Financial Details:** $8,800,000 program budget
- **Eligibility:** commercial and industrial facilities; existing or new construction; facilities used predominantly by low income populations receive 100% project cost reimbursement

## Alaska Housing Finance Corporation
**Source:** http://www.ahfc.us/buy/add-options/energy-efficiency-rate-reduction/
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** The page describes an energy efficiency program offered by Alaska Housing Finance Corporation that provides interest rate reductions on home loans for new or existing energy-efficient homes and properties undergoing improvements. The program offers specific percentage point reductions based on the property's star rating, whether it has access to natural gas, and the number of steps improved in the final energy rating.

### Energy Efficiency Rate Reduction
- **Type:** Low-Interest Loan
- **Financial Details:** -0.250%, -0.375%, -0.125%, -0.250%, -0.500%, -0.625%, -0.375%, -0.500%, -0.750%
- **Eligibility:** Residential, must meet Alaska Building Energy Efficiency Standard (BEES), may qualify if financing new or existing energy-efficient homes or when borrowers make energy improvements to an existing home
- **How to Apply:** Contact an approved lender, the borrower must notify Alaska Housing at the time of application that the borrower intends to participate in the program. Within 365 days of loan closing the borrower must obtain an initial energy rating, complete the improvements, obtain the final energy rating and within 30 days of the final rating submit documentation to the loan servicer.
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** Interest rate reductions apply to the first $250,000 of the loan amount. For loans committed prior to October 1, 2024, the interest rate reduction applies to the first $200,000.

**Source:** http://caec.coop/member-benefits-services/heat-pumps/
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** The page provides details on heat pump rebates offered by Central Alabama Electric Cooperative for both standard and manufactured homes. The rebates vary based on the type of system installed, its SEER rating, and whether it's a new or existing home.

### Heat Pump Rebates for Standard Homes
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $350/ton, $350 per ton for 15 SEER or greater
- **Eligibility:** Standard homes, must have a business license for installing HVAC systems, load calculation required and proof of purchase (invoice) needed
- **Sector:** Residential

### Heat Pump Rebates for New Manufactured Homes
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $400, $600, $700 based on tonnage
- **Eligibility:** New manufactured homes replacing an electric furnace with a heat pump
- **Sector:** Residential

### Heat Pump Rebates for Existing Manufactured Homes
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $400 per ton to the homeowner
- **Eligibility:** Existing manufactured homes converting from an electric furnace to a heat pump, skirting inspection required by CAEC
- **Sector:** Residential

### Heat Pump Rebates for Dual Fuel and Mini-Split Systems in Manufactured Homes
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $350/ton, 14 SEER - $250/ton, 15 SEER – $350 per ton, 16 SEER or greater – $350/ton
- **Eligibility:** Dual fuel and mini-split systems in manufactured homes must meet specific SEER ratings
- **Sector:** Residential

- **Notes:** The cost is initially placed on the power bill but will be refunded when eligible items are purchased.

### Advanced Energy Audit $100
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $100
- **Eligibility:** CAEC members who make home improvements based on the ETC’s recommendation will be reimbursed.
- **How to Apply:** Contact CAEC to schedule an audit. Reimbursement requires submitting receipts for home improvements made within one year of the audit date.
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** The cost is initially placed on the power bill but will be refunded when eligible items are purchased.

### Virtual Audit no cost
- **Type:** Free Service
- **Eligibility:** CAEC members who can perform a virtual audit using phone or video calls.
- **How to Apply:** Contact CAEC to schedule a virtual audit. No additional fees are required.
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** During the audit, the member will walk through the home allowing the ETC to see areas of concern.

**Summary:** The page describes Central Alabama Electric Cooperative's Peak Shaving Program, which aims to reduce peak electricity usage by installing free Peak Shaving Devices (PSDs) on electric water heaters. Participants can also qualify for a water heater rebate.

### Peak Shaving Program
- **Type:** Free Equipment
- **Financial Details:** AT NO COST
- **Eligibility:** Residential customers with an electric water heater; active CAEC member customer
- **How to Apply:** Call (800) 545-5735, ext. 2118 to participate in the free Peak Shaving Program
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** PSD is installed by a licensed electrician at no cost; participants can qualify for a water heater rebate

### Water Heater Rebates
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Eligibility:** Residential customers with an electric water heater; active CAEC member customer
- **How to Apply:** Call (800) 545-5735, ext. 2118 to participate in the free Peak Shaving Program and qualify for a rebate
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** Participants in the Peak Shaving Program can qualify for this rebate

## Central Alabama Electric Cooperative
**Source:** http://caec.coop/member-benefits-services/water-heater-rebates
**Type:** Sublink
**Summary:** The page provides details about the Water Heater Rebate Program offered by Central Alabama Electric Cooperative, including eligibility criteria and application instructions.

### Water Heater Rebate Program
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $235, $375
- **Eligibility:** Residential
Must be a CAEC member
Install an electric water heater with minimum energy factor of .92
Participate in CAEC’s Peak Shaving Program
Keep the Peak Shaving Device (PSD) active for two years
Installed to a 220/240 volt outlet
- **How to Apply:** Email cbrowder@coop.caec.com with completed Water Heater Rebate Form and proof of purchase. Call (800) 545-5735 or Cindy Browder at ext. 2118 or (334) 351-2118 for more information.
- **Sector:** Residential

## CAEC
**Source:** https://caec.coop/news/tips/category/energy-saving-tips/insulationsealing
**Type:** Sublink
**Summary:** This page contains tips for reducing energy loss in homes through sealing air leaks, insulating attics, and using appropriate insulation materials. CAEC offers various services including free energy audits to help members save on their power bills by identifying areas of inefficiency and providing recommendations for improvement.

### Energy audits
- **Type:** free audit
- **Eligibility:** members of CAEC
- **How to Apply:** Contact CAEC for personalized energy-saving recommendations, programs and incentives
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** CAEC offers two styles of audits, the traditional in-person audit conducted by one of their Energy and Technology Consultants (ETC), as well as a virtual audit. Once the energy audit is performed, members will have the opportunity to correct issues in the home based on the ETC’s recommendation. The return on investment comes in the form of savings on your power bill due to reduced energy usage.

### Advanced Audit
- **Type:** refundable fee
- **Financial Details:** $100 refundable after improvements are made within a year
- **Eligibility:** members of CAEC
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** The Advanced Audit is $100 and includes the blower door test, which helps identify air leaks in your home.

### Basic Audit
- **Type:** refundable fee
- **Financial Details:** $75 refundable after improvements are made within a year
- **Eligibility:** members of CAEC
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** The Basic Audit costs $75 and includes everything except the blower door test.

**Source:** https://wavemaker.commerceri.com/information/wavemaker-information
**Type:** Sublink
**Summary:** This page contains information about the Wavemaker Fellowship program in Rhode Island, which offers an annual refundable tax credit against Rhode Island income tax for eligible applicants with student loan repayments. The financial details include caps based on the degree level of the applicant.

### Wavemaker Fellowship Tax Credit
- **Type:** Tax Credit
- **Financial Details:** $6,000 for a Fellow with a Master's degree or higher
$4,000 for a Fellow with a Bachelor’s degree
$1,000 for a Fellow with an Associate’s degree
- **Eligibility:** Have a two-year, four-year or graduate degree from an accredited institution.
Have annual higher education loan repayment expenses.
Work full-time, or have an offer of employment to work full-time, in Rhode Island
Pending eligibility (reviewed in 12-month intervals post-acceptance)
- **How to Apply:** Applications meeting the eligibility requirements will be considered by a Fellowship Committee who evaluates applications on a merit basis and gives priority to certain roles.
- **Notes:** The credit can be applied against Rhode Island income tax or redeemed for the cash equivalent
Refunds of the tax credit may be subject to federal income tax.
Fellows will be chosen on a merit basis by a Fellowship Committee.

## City of Houston
**Source:** http://greenhoustontx.gov/energy.html
**Type:** Sublink
**Summary:** The page describes various initiatives and programs implemented by the City of Houston focusing on energy efficiency, including building performance contracting, LED traffic lights and streetlight conversions, LEED certification for buildings, and LED lighting conversion projects.

### Performance Contracting for COH Buildings
- **Eligibility:** 297 facilities including office buildings, libraries, multi-service centers, police stations, fire stations, health centers, recreation facilities
- **How to Apply:** Utilize two ESCOs-Siemens Building Industries and Schneider Electric
- **Sector:** Commercial
- **Notes:** 5.3 million square feet under assessment, 4.5 million square feet receiving energy conservation measures

### LED Traffic Lights and Streetlight Pilot Project
- **Financial Details:** $10,000 per day or $3.6 million a year in savings
- **Sector:** Commercial
- **Notes:** 2000 intersections completed, average 90% cost savings, 65% energy savings, last longer (7 years vs. 1 year), remaining 400 streetlights complete by 2010

### LEED Certification and Building Retrofit Program
- **Eligibility:** New buildings since 2004, retrofitted City facilities
- **Sector:** Commercial
- **Notes:** 37 LEED buildings, $70 million in energy efficiency retrofits, 6 million square feet of retrofitted facilities saving over 22 million kWh annually

### LED Lighting Conversion Program
- **Sector:** Commercial
- **Notes:** CenterPoint converting ~175,000 streetlights to LED technology reducing City's energy usage by 50%, expected project completion ahead of schedule

## Delmarva Power & Light Company
**Source:** http://homeenergysavings.delmarva.com/home-performance-with-energy-star-program
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** The page describes two programs offered by Delmarva Power & Light Company to improve home energy efficiency: Home Performance with ENERGY STAR® Moderate Income Offering and Home Performance with ENERGY STAR®. Both provide financial incentives such as rebates and low-interest financing for residential property improvements.

### Home Performance with ENERGY STAR® Moderate Income Offering
- **Financial Details:** up to $10,000 in rebates and incentives, up to $5,000 towards a home energy assessment with efficiency recommendations and report. Additionally, the program offers low-interest financing options for qualifying moderate-income homeowners.
- **Eligibility:** qualifying moderate-income homeowners
- **Sector:** Residential

### Home Performance with ENERGY STAR®
- **Financial Details:** $10,000 in rebates and incentives.
- **Eligibility:** homeowners who want to improve their home's energy efficiency
- **How to Apply:** Schedule an assessment. Install qualified products and services. Submit documentation for rebate.
- **Sector:** Residential

**Source:** https://homeenergysavings.delmarva.com/md/residential/hvac-efficiency-program
**Type:** Sublink
**Summary:** The page describes various programs aimed at encouraging residential customers to improve their home's heating and cooling efficiency through rebates for high-efficiency equipment installation and tune-ups. It also mentions financing options for these improvements.

### HVAC Efficiency Program
- **Type:** rebate
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** The program aims to encourage the installation of high-efficiency central air conditioners and heat pumps.

### Ductless Systems Efficiency Program
- **Type:** rebate
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** The program aims to encourage the installation of high-efficiency ductless systems.

### Geothermal Heat Pumps Efficiency Program
- **Type:** rebate
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** The program aims to encourage the installation of high-efficiency geothermal heat pumps.

### HVAC Tune-up Program
- **Type:** rebate
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** The program aims to encourage regular maintenance and tune-ups of existing HVAC systems.

### Financing Options for Efficiency Upgrades
- **Type:** low-interest loan
- **Sector:** Residential
- **Notes:** The program offers financing options to help homeowners afford energy efficiency upgrades, including HVAC systems.

## Pepco
**Source:** http://homeenergysavings.pepco.com/home-performance-with-energy-star-program
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** The page provides information about energy efficiency programs offered by Pepco, focusing on residential customers in Maryland and DC. It includes details about rebates, free home assessments, and financing options for upgrading homes with high-efficiency equipment.

### Home Performance with ENERGY STAR®
- **Type:** rebate
- **Financial Details:** $500 - $4,500 rebates on qualifying upgrades; free home assessment; $150 rebate for air sealing and insulation; free water heater blanket; $300 rebate for adding an electric heat pump (requires air conditioning replacement)
- **Eligibility:** Free home energy assessments are available to Pepco customers who live in Maryland or DC. To be eligible, the customer must either receive their electricity service from Pepco (PEPCO), have a heating system that is 10 years old or older and uses fuel oil, propane, or natural gas, AND has not completed an energy audit within the last 24 months; or qualify for income assistance. For rebates on equipment upgrades, customers must use a participating HVAC contractor.
- **How to Apply:** Customers can apply through the utility's website or by calling the customer service number provided.
- **Sector:** Residential

### Income Eligible Energy Efficiency Program
- **Type:** grant, rebate, on-bill financing
- **Financial Details:** $500 - $4,500 rebates and/or on-bill financing to upgrade homes with high-efficiency equipment and services
- **Sector:** Residential

### Home Performance with ENERGY STAR® Moderate Income Offering
- **Type:** rebate, grant, on-bill financing
- **Financial Details:** $500 - $4,500 rebates and/or on-bill financing to upgrade homes with high-efficiency equipment and services
- **Sector:** Residential

- **Sector:** Commercial, Industrial
- **Notes:** The program is a declining block incentive program where incentive rates decline between each block.
